January 9, 1990
Jru ll
Alice Hughes
Fai.r Politica1 Practices Commission
Travelers Hote1
428 J St., Ste. 800
Sacramento, Calif. r958l4
Dear Alice:
The following is the proposed plan for raising campaign
funds on Marian Bergesonrs behalf.
A weekend fishing trip to Fa11 River Mills, Ca1if. in re-
turn for a $IrOOO.OOO per person contribution to Marian,s
campaign.
Lodging will be in a private resj-dence. This house is
jointly owned by five families. 
The house is never rented
and use is restrj-cted to the partners and guestg, with one
of the partners present while being used.
The primary attraction is the access to the stream. The
house ls located on the river with it's 
own dock and ramp.
The partnership includes numerous boats so there is no out
of pocket expenses relative to lodging or fishing.
Transportation by car wiJ-l be at contributors olJn expense.
For those desiring to f1y, air transportation will be made
available. 
Four of the partners in the house own planes
and generalJ-y f1y up to the house landing at the Fa11 River
Mi11s airport. 
Since we keep a campertruck at the airport
there is no ground transportation expense.
Personally, Bill and I go up there three or four times a
year and there usually are four empty seats in our p1ane,
which raises the question, what part of the expense is to
be considered our in lieu contribution?
Ttre ovners of the other planes who don't go along but provide
transportation will be reimbursed from campaign funds over
the a11owab1e contribution.
f hope this clarifies what we intend to provide. If your
office needs any additional information please 1et me
know.
S incerely,
riz* X**/Zrr-
Bev Langstorl
18 Oakcrest Lane
Newport Beach, Ca, ,92660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester is asking for guidance on how to properly account for in-kind contributions related to a fundraising fishing trip, specifically regarding the value of free airplane transportation and use of a jointly-owned riverside house for campaign fundraising at $1,000,000 per person contribution.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the entire letter is structured as a proposal narrative with an implicit question embedded at the end regarding in-kind contribution valuation. Document lacks FPPC letterhead and response language.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The requester is asking for guidance on how to properly account for in-kind contributions related to a fundraising fishing trip, specifically regarding the value of free airplane transportation and use of a jointly-owned riverside house for campaign fundraising at $1,000,000 per person contribution.

embedding.summary: Bev Langston submits a proposed fundraising plan to Alice Hughes at the FPPC for a campaign event featuring a fishing trip to Fall River Mills, California in exchange for $1,000,000 per person contributions, detailing the in-kind donations of lodging, boats, and air transportation and requesting guidance on contribution valuation.
